name: soft_implementation
narrative: The RGB adopts modest increases of 0-1% for two consecutive years, signaling restraint without a hard freeze. City rhetoric emphasizes tenant protection but avoids alarming capital markets. Landlords absorb short-term margin compression while waiting for a policy reversal.
assumptions: RGB votes 0-1% increases for 1-year leases in 2026 and 2027
No changes to preferential rent or vacancy decontrol thresholds
421-a successor program remains stalled in Albany
Federal interest rates remain elevated (6%+ on 30-year mortgages)
No major new city capital commitments to affordable housing
Property tax assessments continue on existing trajectory

expected_channels.new_construction: Modest decline (5-10%) as developers pause marginal projects
expected_channels.building_maintenance: Slight decline in discretionary maintenance spending
expected_channels.market_rents: Mild upward pressure (+2-3% above baseline) as regulated supply tightens
expected_channels.tenant_turnover: Reduced turnover as tenants lock in below-market rents
expected_channels.capital_investment: Deferred rehab spending, MCI filings decline

name: full_rent_freeze
narrative: The RGB votes a strict 0% increase on both 1-year and 2-year leases for four consecutive years, representing the most aggressive tenant-protection stance since the board's creation. Mayor Mamdani frames this as emergency relief during a cost-of-living crisis. The policy triggers significant behavioral changes across the housing market, with landlords, developers, and lenders all adjusting expectations.
assumptions: RGB votes 0% on all lease renewals for 4 consecutive years (2026-2029)
Vacancy bonus eliminated
Individual Apartment Improvement (IAI) spending cap tightened
Major Capital Improvement (MCI) rent increases capped at 1%
Good Cause Eviction enacted statewide
No new tax abatement program for rental construction
Property taxes continue rising at 3-5% annually
Operating costs (insurance, utilities, labor) rising 4-6% annually
Federal mortgage rates remain above 6%

expected_channels.new_construction: Sharp decline (20-35%) in new multifamily starts, especially in outer boroughs
expected_channels.building_maintenance: Significant deferred maintenance; HPD violations rise 15-25% over 2 years
expected_channels.market_rents: Strong upward pressure (+8-15%) as supply contracts and demand shifts to unregulated stock
expected_channels.property_values: Stabilized building values drop 15-30%; distressed sales increase
expected_channels.tenant_turnover: Near-zero voluntary turnover in stabilized units; warehousing risk
expected_channels.capital_investment: MCI and rehab investment collapses; building systems deteriorate
expected_channels.municipal_finance: Property tax revenue growth slows as assessments decline on stabilized buildings
expected_channels.displacement: Increased harassment complaints as some landlords seek buyouts

name: freeze_plus_buildout
narrative: Combines a 2-year rent freeze with an aggressive city-led affordable housing construction program. The administration pairs tenant protection with supply-side investment, committing $5B+ in capital funds for public and nonprofit housing construction on city-owned land. The theory is that public supply can offset the private-market chilling effect of the freeze.
assumptions: RGB votes 0% for 2 consecutive years (2026-2027), then returns to modest increases
City commits $5B in capital budget for affordable housing construction
City-owned sites fast-tracked for development (streamlined ULURP)
Mandatory inclusionary housing expanded in all rezonings
New public developer entity created (modeled on Montgomery County HOC)
Federal LIHTC allocation increased via state advocacy
Property tax relief for buildings maintaining affordability covenants
Good Cause Eviction enacted
Mortgage rates begin declining toward 5.5% by 2028

expected_channels.new_construction: Private starts decline 15-25%, but public/nonprofit starts add 5,000-8,000 units/year after ramp-up
expected_channels.building_maintenance: Moderate deferred maintenance during freeze years; less severe than pure freeze due to shorter duration
expected_channels.market_rents: Moderate upward pressure (+4-8%) initially, stabilizing as public supply comes online (18-36 month lag)
expected_channels.property_values: Initial decline (10-20%) in stabilized buildings, partial recovery as successor abatement materializes
expected_channels.tenant_turnover: Low turnover during freeze, normalizes after
expected_channels.capital_investment: Private MCI investment declines but partially offset by city-funded rehab programs
expected_channels.municipal_finance: Short-term strain on capital budget; long-term property tax base growth from new public housing
expected_channels.affordability: Net improvement in affordability metrics after 3-4 years as public units deliver
